scopes.config: tool config files (shell, git, nvim, zellij, etc.)

current_goal: Bootstrap kit for a fresh Linux/WSL machine: clone, run install.sh, done.

tasks.title: Probe openSUSE Tumbleweed (then Fedora) via dual-boot
tasks.notes: Decision 2026-07-22: try Tumbleweed first (rolling + snapper rollbacks), Fedora as fallback; Debian testing and Arch ruled out. Dual-boot on this PC or upcoming machine. Follow-up: extend docs/BOOTSTRAP.md and eventually install.sh beyond apt (zypper/dnf). Disk cleanup done 2026-07-22 (C: 15->127 GB free); Windows prepped (BitLocker off, hibernation off, chkdsk clean); Disk Mgmt shrink blocked by NTFS metadata -> resize from installer.

tasks.title: Normalize keybind mnemonics across zellij/nvim/inner tools
tasks.notes: Semantic consistency of the mnemonic letter, not just collision-avoidance: a given letter should mean the same verb everywhere (e.g. m=move in zellij, not 'mark' elsewhere). Goal: lower cognitive load. Follow-up from the zellij quick-terminal/mode-alignment branch. Collision checker exists (scripts/check-keybind-collisions.py); this is about meaning, not overlap. Also revisit nvim-vs-zellij/ghostty collisions (nvim maps only apply in-pane; zellij globals shadow them).

tasks.title: Install lazygit + wire into nvim and broot
tasks.notes: Researched 2026-07-23. Two consumers: (1) nvim plugin — value narrowed to interactive rebase, partial staging, history browse/cherry-pick; everything else covered by alt+f/delegation. (2) broot: conf.hjson already defines a Ctrl-g verb ('lazygit --path {directory}') that errors harmlessly until lazygit is installed. Shared prereq: add install_lazygit to CLI-extras (packaged on zypper; Ubuntu needs a release binary/PPA). LIGHT scope. Deferred for now. See references.

tasks.title: Consider telescope-fzf-native for faster fuzzy sorting
tasks.notes: C-compiled sorter (needs 'make' at build time); enables fzf syntax ('exact, ^prefix, suffix$). Nice-to-have speed boost over default Lua sorter; evaluate if telescope filtering feels slow.
